calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Dec-26-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chris DeWys, by CourtCall, for petitioner.

Mr. DeWys stated that at the hearing on the first petition for confirmation of spousal property, the petitioner presented evidence that two accounts originated with community property funds, and there was only an issue as to one account where the original deposit predated available bank records. He contended that the Court had not addressed the merits of the petition as to the two accounts for which documentation had been provided, but had found that the petition could not be granted as presented because of the inclusion of the third account. The Court responded that the merits of the entire first petition had been considered by the Court, and that the petition had been denied for failure of the petitioner to meet the burden of proof for the petition. Mr. DeWys stated that there had been no issue raised by the Probate Examiner or the Court as to the source of funds for the two accounts being community property. The Court replied that he had ruled upon the merits of the previous petition, and that the present petition was a refiling of the claims previously asserted and ruled upon. He noted that there had been no request for reconsideration, and that the attempt to relitigate the court's prior ruling by filing the new petition was not the proper way to proceed. The petition was denied.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Wan. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Dec-18-2017 Continued To Dec-26-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Oct-31-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Chris Dewys for Marwa Elshawa (Petitioner).

Mr. Dewys stated that they have subpoenaed all of the records they could, that Bank of America does not have any records of the closed account, that they have met all of the local rules and that he does not know what else they can do. Mr. Dewys also provided an alternative request stating that if the Court was hesitant on the third bank account and is inclined to deny the petition, he will withdraw the third account and asked to have the two bank accounts that have no issues approved. Mr. Dewys provided a revised order reflecting only two of the bank accounts. The Petition for Spousal Property is taken under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.
